course_title: Special Topics in Biology: Human as Drivers Evol. Change
subject: BIOL
course_description: Selected topics in Economics. May be repeated for credit when topic varies.
course_notes: Economist's Guide to Thinking with AI AI is quietly rewriting how economists research, write, analyze data, and make policy. This course is about learning to think with AI, not letting it think for you. You will learn how large language models work, including Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, and beyond. You will install Claude, create your own set of Claude skills for Claude Code and Claude Cowork, and build hands-on workflows for literature reviews, writing, data analysis, and policy memos. Just as importantly, you will learn where AI fails and how to spot and prevent errors, biases, and hallucinations before they shape your analysis. No coding background is required. By the end of the semester, you will leave with a documented AI workflow, a personal AI use policy, and the kind of critical judgment that employers, graduate programs, and the economics profession are beginning to expect. Requirements: a personal laptop and Econ 101 and Econ 112. Econ 220 is not required but recommended. You will also need a paid anthropic subscription ($20 per month minimum required).
requisites: (BIOL 142 and BIOL 142L) or BIOL_OX 142WE or equivalent transfer credit as a prerequisite.
